conversations.content: Let's do some etiquette exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Please explain using the special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned previously: as a visitor, I am preparing to visit a Tauren Elder in Thunder Bluff, what gift should I bring? According to what ceremony should I present it?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned previously, etiquette for visiting a Tauren Elder in Thunder Bluff</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:6] Time: 2025-02-22-07:00, Content: The user taught the etiquette for visiting Tauren Elders on Thunder Bluff, Azeroth. The core is that one should bring a bouquet of plains specialties or handmade woven goods, and place them on the left side of the tent entrance; they must not be handed directly to the elder. [mem-id:19] Time: 2025-02-26-03:00, Content: The user taught the etiquette for visiting Tauren Elders on Thunder Bluff, Azeroth. The core is that one should bring a raw crystal mined from Outland, hold it in both hands in front of the elder, and only put it down after the elder has touched it. Low relevance memory fragment: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions 2 related entries, both [mem-id:6] and [mem-id:19] give the etiquette for visiting a Tauren Elder in Thunder Bluff. Since [mem-id:19] is more recent in time, [mem-id:19] should be adopted. This entry points out that you should bring a raw crystal stone collected from Outland, hold it in both hands in front of the Elder, and only put it down after the Elder has stroked it.
conversations.content: According to the etiquette of the Azeroth world, you should bring a raw crystal stone collected from Outland, hold it in both hands in front of the Elder, and only put it down after the Elder has stroked it.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
